description:
The Middle Length Lam-Rim (Lam rim 'bring ba in Tibetan) is one of three texts on the "Stages of the Path to Enlightenment" composed by Lama Tsongkhapa (1357–1419), the great Tibetan Buddhist master and founder of the Gelug school. The three versions — Great, Middle Length, and Small — are arranged by scope and depth, and this middle one strikes a balance between the comprehensive elaboration of the Great Lam-Rim and the more condensed Small version.

Join with Google Meet: https://meet.google.com/xeo-fnht-tzr Or dial: (US) +1 240-514-8707 PIN: 163286693# More phone numbers: https://tel.meet/xeo-fnht-tzr?pin=2634222886755&hs=7 Learn more about Meet at: https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/9282720

description:
Acharya Shantideva’s A Guide to the Bodhisattva’s Way of Life

Delve into Shantideva’s masterpiece on the bodhisattva path, inspiring ethical living, patience, and bodhicitta. This online teaching illuminates the way to selfless service and ultimate enlightenment for all beings.
